explicit_text: I was born in Kansas City, Missouri, at Saint Luke's Hospital where my grandfather worked as an insurance salesman. My family had strong ties to the area, with my mother’s relatives and my father’s education connected to the region. Later, after living in Wyoming, we moved back to Missouri, and I attended school in Carrollton, which is near Kansas City. The city and its surroundings have been a significant part of my family’s history and my upbringing.

explicit_text: During World War II, my father, George Weymouth Bailey, served overseas and was part of an expense-paid tour of the Mediterranean with General Patton. While he was away, my family lived with my grandparents in Kansas City. The war shaped much of our family’s movements and my father’s career path afterward. It was a defining event that influenced where we lived and how we grew up.

explicit_text: My father, George Weymouth Bailey, was a dedicated family dentist who grew up in Sheridan, Wyoming. He attended William Jewell College in Missouri, as did many of my mother’s relatives, which is how my parents met. After serving in World War II and touring with General Patton, he established a dental practice in Kemmerer, Wyoming, before moving back to Missouri. He was a hardworking man with a fixed routine, always on call for his patients, and deeply respected in his community until his tragic death at Shangri-La Marina.

We lived there until my mother’s mother developed rheumatoid arthritis, which prompted us to move back to Missouri. Kemmerer represents my early childhood and my father’s professional beginnings.

explicit_text: Shangri-La Marina is where my father tragically died in a car accident about 25 years ago. He missed a turn at the marina, and his car went into the water on a dark, rainy night. Despite efforts to rescue him, he drowned, which was a devastating loss for our family. The marina later added fencing to prevent such accidents, and it remains a poignant place in our family history.

Grove became part of the story of my father’s final moments and our family’s response to the tragedy.
